name: Nifty 50
summary: Day 68 Wednesday. **NIFTY UNDERPERFORMS THE CRISIS-RELIEF REGIME — +0.2% TO 24,082.50 vs KOSPI +6.7%, MSCI EM +2.0%, DAX +1.7%, S&P +0.8%.** Indian equities are the WORST POSITIONED for this risk-on regime among major markets despite cheaper relative valuation. Decomposition: (1) FII outflows continued with ₹3,200+ crore session outflow vs DII absorption capacity testing; capital-flow curb regime (NOP/NDF) constrains NRI hedging activity; (2) IT sector lagging — TCS, Infosys, Wipro under pressure as Apple's chip-talks news redirected AI flows to semiconductor manufacturers (Korea/Taiwan/US) rather than IT services; (3) Bank Nifty +0.6% to 54,867.65 modest recovery; Adani complex still digesting Hindenburg/SEBI overhang; (4) Hormuz remains operationally closed (NOT diplomatically resolved) — Indian oil import logistics still constrained; OMC stocks (IOC, BPCL, HPCL) capped despite Brent collapse to $107.96. **For deployment thesis:** validates bifurcation thesis from May 3-5 briefs. Indian equities are CHEAP relative to crisis fundamentals (still down -7% on 3M while Korea +45%, Taiwan +29%) but require domestic catalyst (RBI cut, fiscal stimulus, FII rotation reversal) to outperform. The 200-DMA at ~24,500 capping; structural breakout requires ₹93-handle on USD/INR. FII CY26 outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore (~$23B+) already exceeds CY25 full-year ₹1.66T. FPI ownership ~16% (2-decade low, BELOW domestic for first time); DII (SIP-driven) absorbed ~90%. The DII-finite-absorption risk: if FII outflows extend beyond ₹2.5 lakh crore CY26 without DII matching, Nifty support breaks structurally.

current.deployment_zone: Underperforming risk-on regime; structural breakout requires ₹93-handle USD/INR + domestic catalyst. 24,500 200-DMA capping. Below 23,800 = structural breach; cheap on valuation but flows-constrained.
